# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

#!/usr/bin/env ruby


require 'open3'

params[:size] ||= "10x10"
params["image"] ||= "test.png"  # this is a test file located in the public directory which is mounted as a virtual filesystem under /images

command = "/usr/local/bin/convert #{params['image']} -resize '#{params[:size]}' #{params['format']}-#{params["image"]}"

result, status = Open3.capture2e(command) 

if status != 0 
  puts "Error with command" + command
  exit!(-1)  # this is a system exit with a return code indicating an error occurred. In ruby, any non-zero return code indicates there was an error. So anything but zero means there's an error.
  # the open3 library automatically catches signals (like ctrl+c), sets the status of the process to "exited" and returns its return value in the result. In this case 256 means 2^8, which is an error code, not actually an error. That's why it's -1
end

puts "Converted!"

Code:<|start_of_text|>
